translations.descricao: Pneumonia is an acute infection of the pulmonary parenchyma caused by bacteria, viruses, or fungi, representing one of the main causes of respiratory morbidity and mortality. It can be classified as community-acquired (CAP), hospital-acquired (HAP), or associated with mechanical ventilation (VAP). In children, it is a common cause of hospitalization. In the Brazilian context, management follows protocols for respiratory infections.
translations.sus.indicacao: Clinical management of acute respiratory infections, including pneumonia as a complication of influenza or in primary care for mild cases.
translations.sus.populacaoAlvo: Adults and children with acute respiratory symptoms, prioritizing vulnerable groups such as the elderly and immunocompromised.
translations.sociedadesMedicas.indicacao: Diagnosis and empirical treatment of CAP in adults with symptoms such as fever, cough, and pulmonary infiltrates; for HAP/VAP, coverage for multidrug-resistant pathogens; in children >3 months, etiological evaluation.
translations.epidemiologia.prevalencia: Pneumonia affects about 5-11 cases per 1,000 adults/year globally for CAP; in children, it is prevalent in <5 years with rates of 0.5-2%. In Brazil, it is estimated 1-2 million annual cases of CAP.
translations.epidemiologia.incidencia: Global incidence of pneumonia is 150 million cases/year, with 4-5 million deaths; for VAP, 10-20% of ventilated patients. In Brazil, CAP incidence is 3-5/1,000 inhabitants/year.
translations.epidemiologia.mortalidade: Mortality from CAP in adults is 5-7% in hospitalized patients; for VAP, 20-40%; in children, <1% with adequate treatment. In Brazil, pneumonia is the 4th cause of death from respiratory diseases.
